title: Idioms & Expressions Set 1 — Office Life
description: Master 30 essential English idioms used daily in offices. Learn when to use idioms naturally in professional conversations — from casual chats to team meetings.
descriptionTr: Ofislerde gunluk kullanilan 30 temel Ingilizce deyimi ogren. Profesyonel konusmalarda deyimleri dogal sekilde kullanmayi ogren — sohbetten ekip toplantilarinda.

vocabulary.example: Let's get the ball rolling on the new marketing campaign.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni pazarlama kampanyasini baslatmaya baslayalim.
vocabulary.extra: Who wants to get the ball rolling with the first presentation?
vocabulary.extraTr: Ilk sunumla isi kim baslatmak ister?

vocabulary.turkish: ayni fikirde olmak / ayni noktada olmak
vocabulary.example: Before we proceed, let's make sure we're all on the same page.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Devam etmeden once hepimizin ayni fikirde oldugundan emin olalim.
vocabulary.extra: I want to make sure the client and our team are on the same page.
vocabulary.extraTr: Musterinin ve ekibimizin ayni noktada oldugundan emin olmak istiyorum.

vocabulary.example: We need to think outside the box to solve this logistics problem.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu lojistik sorunu cozmek icin kalibin disinda dusurmemiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: The CEO encouraged everyone to think outside the box during the brainstorm.
vocabulary.extraTr: CEO beyin firtinasi sirasinda herkesi yaratici dusunmeye tesvik etti.

vocabulary.example: The deal fell through, so we're back to square one.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Anlasma bozuldu, bu yuzden basa donduk.
vocabulary.extra: If this prototype fails, we'll be back to square one.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu prototip basarisiz olursa, sifira donecegiz.

vocabulary.example: We can't cut corners on product safety — it's non-negotiable.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Urun guvenliginde kisa yoldan gidemeyiz — tartismaya acik degil.
vocabulary.extra: The supplier was cutting corners, and the quality dropped significantly.
vocabulary.extraTr: Tedarikci kaliteden kisiyordu ve kalite onemli olcude dustu.

vocabulary.example: The bottom line is we need to increase revenue by 20%.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sonuc olarak geliri %20 artirmamiz gerekiyor.
vocabulary.extra: Forget the details — what's the bottom line?
vocabulary.extraTr: Detaylari birak — sonuc ne?

vocabulary.turkish: her iki taraf icin de kazanc
vocabulary.example: This partnership is a win-win situation for both companies.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu ortaklik her iki sirket icin de kazanc.
vocabulary.extra: We negotiated a win-win deal with the logistics provider.
vocabulary.extraTr: Lojistik saglayiciyla her iki tarafin da kazandigi bir anlasma muzakere ettik.

vocabulary.example: Please keep me in the loop about the project status.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Lutfen beni proje durumu hakkinda bilgilendirmeye devam edin.
vocabulary.extra: Make sure you keep the stakeholders in the loop at all times.
vocabulary.extraTr: Paydaslari her zaman gelismelerden haberdar ettiginizden emin olun.

vocabulary.turkish: kisa bir gorusme yapmak / iletisime gecmek
vocabulary.example: Let's touch base next week to review the progress.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ilerlemeyi gozden gecirmek icin gelecek hafta kisa bir gorusme yapalim.
vocabulary.extra: I'll touch base with the client before the deadline.
vocabulary.extraTr: Son tarihten once musteriyle iletisime gececegim.

vocabulary.turkish: son durumu ogrenmek / bilgi seviyesine gelmek
vocabulary.example: I've been on vacation — can you help me get up to speed?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Tatildeydim — son durumu ogrenmeme yardim edebilir misin?
vocabulary.extra: The new hire needs a week to get up to speed on our systems.
vocabulary.extraTr: Yeni calisan sistemlerimiz hakkinda bilgi seviyesine gelmek icin bir haftaya ihtiyac duyuyor.

vocabulary.turkish: hemen ise koyulmak / hizli baslangic yapmak
vocabulary.example: She hit the ground running on her first day and impressed everyone.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Ilk gununide hemen ise koyuldu ve herkesi etkiledi.
vocabulary.extra: We need someone who can hit the ground running — no time for training.
vocabulary.extraTr: Hemen ise koyulabilecek birine ihtiyacimiz var — egitim icin zaman yok.

vocabulary.example: There's always a learning curve when you switch to a new software.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Yeni bir yazilima gectiginizde her zaman bir ogrenme sureci vardir.
vocabulary.extra: The learning curve for this tool is steep, but it's worth it.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu aracin ogrenme sureci dik ama buna deger.

vocabulary.example: Our support team always goes the extra mile for clients.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Destek ekibimiz musteriler icin her zaman ekstra caba gosteriyor.
vocabulary.extra: If you go the extra mile, management will definitely notice.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ekstra caba gosterirsen, yonetim kesinlikle fark edecek.

vocabulary.example: We burned the midnight oil to finish the proposal before the deadline.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Teklifi son tarihten once bitirmek icin gece gec saatlere kadar calistik.
vocabulary.extra: I don't want the team burning the midnight oil every week — that's not sustainable.
vocabulary.extraTr: Ekibin her hafta gece gec saatlere kadar calismasini istemiyorum — bu surdurulebilir degil.

vocabulary.example: We've been in this meeting for three hours — let's call it a day.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Uc saattir bu toplantidayiz — bugunluk yeter diyelim.
vocabulary.extra: It's already 7 PM. I think we should call it a day.
vocabulary.extraTr: Saat zaten aksam 7. Bence bugunluk bitmeli.

vocabulary.example: We have three new product launches in the pipeline for Q3.
vocabulary.exampleTr: 3. ceyrek icin hazirlanan uc yeni urun lansmanımız var.
vocabulary.extra: There are several deals in the pipeline that could close this month.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu ay kapanabilecek hazirlanmakta olan birkac anlasma var.

vocabulary.example: He pulled some strings to get the meeting with the director.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Direktorle toplanti ayarlamak icin baglantilarini kullandi.
vocabulary.extra: I don't like pulling strings — I'd rather earn it through hard work.
vocabulary.extraTr: Torpil kullanmayi sevmiyorum — siki calisarak hak etmeyi tercih ederim.

vocabulary.turkish: anlık karar vermek / duruma gore hareket etmek
vocabulary.example: We don't have a fixed agenda — let's just play it by ear.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sabit bir gundemimiz yok — duruma gore hareket edelim.
vocabulary.extra: I'm not sure how the client will react, so we'll play it by ear.
vocabulary.extraTr: Musterinin nasil tepki vereceginden emin degilim, bu yuzden duruma gore hareket edecegiz.

vocabulary.example: Can you give me a ballpark figure for the project budget?
vocabulary.exampleTr: Proje butcesi icin yaklasik bir rakam verebilir misin?
vocabulary.extra: I'd say the cost is around $50,000, but that's just a ballpark figure.
vocabulary.extraTr: Maliyetin yaklasik 50.000 dolar oldugunu soyleyebilirim ama bu sadece tahmini bir rakam.

vocabulary.example: I told a joke to break the ice at the beginning of the meeting.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Toplantinin basinda ortami isitmak icin bir saka anlattim.
vocabulary.extra: Small talk is a great way to break the ice with new colleagues.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kisa sohbet yeni meslektaslarla buzlari kirmak icin harika bir yol.

vocabulary.example: Our competitors raised the bar with their new product launch.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Rakiplerimiz yeni urun lansmanlarıyla standardi yukseltti.
vocabulary.extra: We need to raise the bar on customer service quality.
vocabulary.extraTr: Musteri hizmet kalitesinde standardi yukseltmemiz gerekiyor.

vocabulary.example: This AI tool has been a real game changer for our workflow.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bu yapay zeka araci is akisimiz icin gercek bir oyun degistirici oldu.
vocabulary.extra: Remote work was a game changer for employee satisfaction.
vocabulary.extraTr: Uzaktan calisma calisan memnuniyeti icin buyuk bir yenilik oldu.

vocabulary.word: take it with a grain of salt
vocabulary.turkish: ihtiyatla karsilamak / her seyi oldugu gibi kabul etmemek
vocabulary.example: He tends to exaggerate, so take his numbers with a grain of salt.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Abartma egilimi var, bu yuzden rakamlarini ihtiyatla karsila.
vocabulary.extra: Online reviews should be taken with a grain of salt.
vocabulary.extraTr: Cevrimici degerlendirmeler ihtiyatla karsilanmali.

vocabulary.example: We need to bite the bullet and invest in new equipment.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gozumuzu kararlamali ve yeni ekipmana yatirim yapmaliyiz.
vocabulary.extra: After months of hesitation, they finally bit the bullet and hired a consultant.
vocabulary.extraTr: Aylarca tereddut ettikten sonra sonunda gozlerini kararlayip bir danismanni ise aldilar.

vocabulary.example: The marketing and sales teams don't always see eye to eye.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Pazarlama ve satis ekipleri her zaman hemfikir olmuyor.
vocabulary.extra: We finally see eye to eye on the budget allocation.
vocabulary.extraTr: Butce tahsisi konusunda sonunda hemfikir olduk.

vocabulary.example: Don't get lost in details — focus on the big picture.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Detaylarda kaybolma — buyuk resme odaklan.
vocabulary.extra: When you look at the big picture, this quarter was actually successful.
vocabulary.extraTr: Buyuk resme baktiginda, bu ceyrek aslinda basariliydi.

vocabulary.turkish: hata yapmak / firsat kacirmak / sorumluluğu yerine getirememek
vocabulary.example: We dropped the ball on the client follow-up, and they went to a competitor.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Musteri takibinde hata yaptik ve rakibe gittiler.
vocabulary.extra: Don't drop the ball on this deadline — it's critical.
vocabulary.extraTr: Bu son tarihi kacirma — cok onemli.

vocabulary.example: The presentation? It was a piece of cake — I've done it a hundred times.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Sunum mu? Cocuk oyuncagiydi — yuz kere yaptim.
vocabulary.extra: Setting up the new system was a piece of cake with the manual.
vocabulary.extraTr: Kilavuzla yeni sistemi kurmak cok kolaydi.

vocabulary.word: at the end of the day
vocabulary.example: At the end of the day, what matters most is customer satisfaction.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Gunun sonunda en onemli olan musteri memnuniyetidir.
vocabulary.extra: At the end of the day, we all want the project to succeed.
vocabulary.extraTr: Neticede hepimiz projenin basarili olmasini istiyoruz.

vocabulary.turkish: sahne arkasinda / perde arkasinda / gorunmeyen yerde
vocabulary.example: A lot of work happens behind the scenes before a product launch.
vocabulary.exampleTr: Bir urun lansmanindan once perde arkasinda cok is yapilir.
vocabulary.extra: The operations team works behind the scenes to keep everything running.
vocabulary.extraTr: Operasyon ekibi her seyin islemesini saglamak icin perde arkasinda calisir.

grammar.title: Idioms Usage — Meaning, Context & Register
grammar.titleTr: Deyim Kullanimi — Anlam, Baglam & Resmiyet Seviyesi
grammar.explanation: Idioms are fixed expressions whose meaning cannot be understood from the individual words. They make your English sound natural and fluent. However, you need to know WHEN to use them — some are casual (team chats), some work in semi-formal contexts (meetings), and very few belong in formal writing (contracts, legal). This lesson teaches you to use idioms like a native speaker in the right context.
grammar.explanationTr: Deyimler, anlami tek tek kelimelerden anlasilamayan sabit ifadelerdir. Ingilizceni dogal ve akici yapar. Ama NE ZAMAN kullanacagini bilmen gerekiyor — bazilari gunluk (ekip sohbetleri), bazilari yari-resmi baglamlarda (toplantilar) ise yarar ve cok azi resmi yazismada (sozlesmeler, hukuki) kullanilir. Bu ders sana deyimleri dogru baglamda anadil konusmaci gibi kullanmayi ogretiyor.
table: Casual idioms — everyday office talk
Let's call it a day. / That was a piece of cake.
Use freely in: team Slack, casual meetings, lunch chats.
These sound friendly and relaxed — avoid in formal emails.
Semi-formal idioms — meetings & presentations
Let's get the ball rolling. / We need to raise the bar.
Acceptable in: team meetings, client calls, presentations.
Professional but natural — most office idioms fall here.
Register awareness — formal vs casual
Formal: 'Let's initiate the process.' vs Casual: 'Let's get the ball rolling.'
Know both versions — use plain language in contracts, idioms in meetings.
Mixing registers sounds unprofessional — match your audience.
Idioms with negative meaning — use carefully
Cut corners / Drop the ball / Back to square one
These imply criticism — be careful when directing at colleagues.
Say 'We dropped the ball' (team) not 'You dropped the ball' (accusation).
Idiom clusters — related expressions
Planning: in the pipeline, get the ball rolling, a ballpark figure
Progress: hit the ground running, raise the bar, go the extra mile
Group idioms by topic to remember them more easily.
Literal vs idiomatic meaning
'Break the ice' does NOT mean breaking actual ice!
Context tells you: 'Let's break the ice' at a meeting = warm up the group.
If an expression doesn't make literal sense, it's probably an idiom.
When NOT to use idioms
Legal documents, technical specs, compliance reports
Plain language: 'We need to start.' NOT 'Let's get the ball rolling.'
Rule: if the reader might not be a native speaker, use plain language.

tips.en: Learn idioms in PAIRS of opposite meaning: 'raise the bar' (increase standards) vs 'cut corners' (lower standards).
tips.tr: Deyimleri ZIT ANLAMLI ciftler halinde ogren: 'raise the bar' (standardi yukselt) vs 'cut corners' (kaliteden kis).

tips.en: Don't mix idioms! 'Let's get the ball running' is WRONG. It's 'get the ball rolling' OR 'hit the ground running.' Never combine.
tips.tr: Deyimleri karistirma! 'Let's get the ball running' YANLIS. 'Get the ball rolling' VEYA 'hit the ground running.' Asla birlestirme.

tips.en: Use 'at the end of the day' sparingly — it's overused. In writing, prefer 'ultimately' or 'in conclusion.'
tips.tr: 'At the end of the day' az kullan — cok fazla kullaniliyor. Yazida 'ultimately' veya 'in conclusion' tercih et.

tips.en: When someone says 'give me a ballpark figure,' they want a ROUGH estimate. Don't spend hours calculating the exact number.
tips.tr: Biri 'give me a ballpark figure' dediginde, KABA bir tahmin istiyor. Saatlerce kesin rakami hesaplama.

tips.en: In international teams, avoid too many idioms — non-native speakers may not understand them. Use 1-2 per conversation, not 10.
tips.tr: Uluslararasi ekiplerde cok fazla deyim kullanma — anadil konusmacisi olmayanlar anlamayabilir. Konusmada 1-2 kullan, 10 degil.

tips.en: 'Keep me in the loop' is the most useful office idiom. Use it in emails: 'Please keep me in the loop on any updates.'
tips.tr: 'Keep me in the loop' en kullanisli ofis deyimidir. E-postalarda kullan: 'Please keep me in the loop on any updates.'

reading.text:
OFFICE IDIOMS THAT CONFUSE NON-NATIVE SPEAKERS

When I first started working in an international company, my English grammar was decent, but I kept getting confused in meetings. My manager would say things like 'Let's get the ball rolling' and I'd look around wondering what ball she was talking about. Another colleague once told me to 'touch base later,' and I honestly thought it had something to do with baseball.

The truth is, native English speakers use idioms constantly — especially in office environments. A study found that the average professional uses 5 to 10 idioms per business meeting without even realizing it. For non-native speakers, this can feel like a secret code.

The most confusing ones? 'Think outside the box' confused me because I couldn't see any box. 'Burn the midnight oil' sounded dangerous. And when my director said 'Let's play it by ear,' I thought she was making a joke about music.

Here's what I learned: idioms are not random — they follow patterns. Business idioms often come from sports ('drop the ball,' 'hit the ground running'), everyday life ('a piece of cake,' 'break the ice'), or old traditions ('burn the midnight oil' comes from studying by oil lamp).

The key is context. If your manager says 'Let's call it a day' at 5 PM, she means let's stop working. If someone says 'give me a ballpark figure,' they want a rough estimate, not an exact number. And if you hear 'we're back to square one,' the project has started over.

My advice? Don't try to learn all idioms at once. Focus on 5-6 per week, use them in real conversations, and soon they'll feel natural. At the end of the day, idioms are what make you sound like a real professional — not just someone who speaks correct English.
reading.textTr:
ANA DILI INGILIZCE OLMAYANLARI SASIRTAN OFIS DEYIMLERI

Uluslararasi bir sirkette calismaya basladigimda, Ingilizce dilbilgim iyiydi ama toplantilarda surekli karisiyordum. Muduruum 'Let's get the ball rolling' gibi seyler soylerdi ve ben de etrafima bakip hangi toptan bahsettigini merak ederdim. Baska bir meslektasim bir kez 'touch base later' dedi ve durst olarak beyzbolla ilgili oldugunu dusundum.

Gercek su ki, anadili Ingilizce olanlar surekli deyim kullaniyor — ozellikle ofis ortamlarinda. Bir arastirma, ortalama bir profesyonelin is toplantisi basina farkinda bile olmadan 5 ila 10 deyim kullandigini buldu. Anadili Ingilizce olmayanlar icin bu gizli bir sifre gibi hissedilebilir.

En kafa karistiricilar? 'Think outside the box' beni sasirtti cunku hicbir kutu goremiyordum. 'Burn the midnight oil' tehlikeli geldi. Ve direktorom 'Let's play it by ear' dediginde muzikle ilgili saka yaptigini dusundum.

Ogrendigim su: deyimler rastgele degil — kaliplari takip ediyor. Is deyimleri genellikle spordan ('drop the ball,' 'hit the ground running'), gunluk hayattan ('a piece of cake,' 'break the ice') veya eski geleneklerden ('burn the midnight oil' yag lambasiyla calismaktan gelir) turetilir.

Anahtar baglamdir. Mudurun saat 17'de 'Let's call it a day' derse, calisimayi birakalim demektir. Biri 'give me a ballpark figure' derse, kesin sayi degil yaklasik tahmin ister. Ve 'we're back to square one' duyarsan, proje bastan baslamistir.

Tavsiyem? Tum deyimleri bir anda ogrenmeye calisma. Haftada 5-6'sina odaklan, gercek konusmalarda kullan ve kisa surede dogal hissedecekler. Gunun sonunda, deyimler seni gercek bir profesyonel gibi gosteren seylerdir — sadece dogru Ingilizce konusan biri degil.

questions.question: What confused the writer most when they started working internationally?
questions.questionTr: Yazar uluslararasi calismaya basladiginda onu en cok ne sasirtti?

questions.question: How many idioms does the average professional use per business meeting?
questions.questionTr: Ortalama bir profesyonel is toplantisi basina kac deyim kullaniyor?

questions.question: According to the text, where do many business idioms come from?
questions.questionTr: Metne gore bircok is deyimi nereden geliyor?
options: Sports, everyday life, and old traditions

questions.question: What does the writer recommend for learning idioms?
questions.questionTr: Yazar deyim ogrenmek icin ne oneriyor?
options: Focus on 5-6 per week and use them

questions.question: What does 'give me a ballpark figure' mean?
questions.questionTr: 'Give me a ballpark figure' ne anlama gelir?

dialog.title: Idioms in Action — Eyup's Office Day
dialog.context: Eyup'un bir is gununde dogal ofis konusmalarinda deyim kullanimi: sabah ekip toplantisi (Selin, yonetici), ogle arasi sohbet (Kerem, meslektas), ve ogunsonrasi proje degerlendirmesi (Lisa, musteri).

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good morning, team. We have a lot to cover today, so let's get the ball rolling. First, the product launch — where are we?
lines.textTr: Gunaydin ekip. Bugun ele alacak cok isimiz var, o yuzden isi baslatalim. Ilk olarak, urun lansmani — neredeyiz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Morning, Selin. The design phase is done and the development is in the pipeline. We should be ready for testing by next Friday.
lines.textTr: Gunaydin, Selin. Tasarim asamasi tamamlandi ve gelistirme hazirlaniyor. Gelecek Cumaya kadar test icin hazir olmaliyiz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Great. And the marketing budget — can you give me a ballpark figure?
lines.textTr: Harika. Peki pazarlama butcesi — bana yaklasik bir rakam verebilir misin?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Sure. I'd say around $35,000, but I need to touch base with the finance team to confirm the exact amount.
lines.textTr: Tabii. Yaklasik 35.000 dolar derim ama kesin tutari dogrulamak icin finans ekibiyle gorusmem gerekiyor.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That works. Also, I want to make sure everyone's on the same page about the timeline. No cutting corners on quality — we raised the bar last quarter and we need to keep it there.
lines.textTr: Olur. Ayrica herkesin zaman cizelgesi konusunda ayni noktada oldugundan emin olmak istiyorum. Kaliteden kisma yok — gecen ceyrek standardi yukselttik ve orada tutmamiz gerekiyor.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely. The new features are going to be a real game changer for our users. I'll keep you in the loop on every milestone.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle. Yeni ozellikler kullanicilarimiz icin gercek bir oyun degistirici olacak. Her kilometre tasinda sizi bilgilendirmeye devam edecegim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Perfect. Let's touch base again on Wednesday. Meeting adjourned.
lines.textTr: Mukemmel. Carsamba tekrar goruselim. Toplanti kapatildi.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hey Eyup, how was the meeting? I heard Selin has some big plans for the launch.
lines.textTr: Selam Eyup, toplanti nasıl gecti? Selin'in lansman icin buyuk planlari oldugunu duydum.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Yeah, it went well. She wants us to think outside the box for the marketing strategy. No more doing the same old thing.
lines.textTr: Evet, iyi gecti. Pazarlama stratejisi icin kalibin disinda dusunmemizi istiyor. Artik ayni eski seyleri yapmak yok.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Makes sense. Remember last quarter when we dropped the ball on the social media campaign? We ended up back to square one.
lines.textTr: Mantikli. Gecen ceyrek sosyal medya kampanyasinda hata yaptigimizi hatirliyor musun? Basa donduk.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Don't remind me. We were burning the midnight oil for two weeks straight trying to fix it. This time I want to hit the ground running.
lines.textTr: Hatirlatma. Onu duzeltmeye calisarak iki hafta boyunca gece gec saatlere kadar calistik. Bu sefer hemen ise koyulmak istiyorum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Good plan. I also think we need to go the extra mile on customer research. Take the last focus group results with a grain of salt though — the sample size was too small.
lines.textTr: Iyi plan. Musteri arastirmasi konusunda ekstra caba gostermemiz gerektigini de dusunuyorum. Son odak grubu sonuclarini ihtiyatla karsila — orneklem buyuklugu cok kucuktu.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Agreed. At the end of the day, it's the real customer feedback that matters, not just surveys. Hey, should we grab coffee before the afternoon session?
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum. Gunun sonunda onemli olan anketler degil gercek musteri geri bildirimidir. Hey, ogleden sonraki oturumdan once kahve alalim mi?

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Sure, let's do it. We can play it by ear for the afternoon — I don't think we have a fixed agenda.
lines.textTr: Tabii, yapalim. Ogleden sonra icin duruma gore hareket ederiz — sabit bir gundemimiz oldugunu sanmiyorum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Hi Eyup, thanks for the call. I wanted to touch base about the project status. Are we still on track for the June delivery?
lines.textTr: Merhaba Eyup, arama icin tesekkurler. Proje durumu hakkinda kisa bir gorusme yapmak istedim. Haziran teslimatı icin hala yolda miyiz?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Hi Lisa. Yes, we're on track. There's been a steep learning curve with the new platform, but the team adapted quickly. I'd say the integration was a piece of cake compared to what we expected.
lines.textTr: Merhaba Lisa. Evet, yoldayiz. Yeni platformla dik bir ogrenme sureci oldu ama ekip hizla uyum sagladi. Entegrasyonun bekledigimize gore cocuk oyuncagi oldugunu soyleyebilirim.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: That's good to hear. The bottom line for us is that the system needs to be live by June 15th. Can you see the big picture from our side?
lines.textTr: Bunu duymak guzel. Bizim icin sonuc olarak sistemin 15 Haziran'a kadar canli olmasi gerekiyor. Bizim tarafimizdan buyuk resmi gorebiliyor musun?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Absolutely, Lisa. I see the big picture. We had to bite the bullet and rebuild one module from scratch, but it was the right decision. It's a win-win — better performance for you, and a cleaner codebase for us.
lines.textTr: Kesinlikle, Lisa. Buyuk resmi goruyorum. Gozumuzu kararlamali ve bir modulu sifirdan yeniden insa etmemiz gerekti, ama dogru karardi. Herkes icin kazanc — sizin icin daha iyi performans, bizim icin daha temiz kod tabani.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: I appreciate the transparency. A lot of the hard work happens behind the scenes, and I know your team has been going the extra mile. Please keep me in the loop if anything changes.
lines.textTr: Seffafligi takdir ediyorum. Zor isin cogu perde arkasinda oluyor ve ekibinizin ekstra caba gosterdigini biliyorum. Bir sey degisirse lutfen beni bilgilendirmeye devam edin.

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Of course. And if any new requirements come up, we don't need to pull strings — just let me know directly and we'll get up to speed fast. I think we should call it a day on this call — we're on the same page.
lines.textTr: Tabii ki. Ve yeni gereksinimler cikarsa, baglantilari kullanmamiza gerek yok — bana dogrudan soylersin ve hizla son duruma geliriz. Bu aramayi bitirelim bence — ayni noktadayiz.

lines.speaker: A
lines.text: Agreed. You really do see eye to eye with our expectations. Talk soon, Eyup!
lines.textTr: Katiliyorum. Beklentilerimizle gercekten hemfikirsin. Yakindan gorusuruz, Eyup!

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: Thanks, Lisa. Talk soon! That was a productive day — we really raised the bar today.
lines.textTr: Tesekkurler, Lisa. Yakindan gorusuruz! Verimli bir gundu — bugun gercekten standardi yukselttik.

questions.question: What idiom did Selin use to start the meeting?
questions.questionTr: Selin toplantiyi baslatmak icin hangi deyimi kullandi?

questions.question: What happened last quarter that Kerem mentioned?
questions.questionTr: Kerem'in bahsettigi gecen ceyrek ne oldu?
options: They broke the ice with a new client
They dropped the ball on a social media campaign
They pulled strings for a promotion

questions.question: What does Lisa say is 'the bottom line' for her company?
questions.questionTr: Lisa sirketleri icin 'sonuc olarak' ne oldugunu soyluyor?

questions.question: Why did Eyup's team 'bite the bullet'?
options: They rebuilt one module from scratch

questions.question: How many different conversations does Eyup have in this dialog?
questions.questionTr: Eyup bu diyalogda kac farkli konusma yapiyor?

fillBlanks.sentence: Let's get the ball ___ on the new project.
Before we make a decision, let's make sure we're all on the same ___.
We need to think outside the ___ to find a creative solution.
The prototype failed, so we're back to ___ one.
Don't cut ___ on quality — it will cost us later.
The ___ line is that we need more revenue.
Please keep me in the ___ about any updates.
Can you give me a ___ figure for the budget?
I told a joke to break the ___ at the start of the meeting.
At the end of the ___, customer satisfaction is what matters.
The team has been burning the midnight ___ to finish on time.
fillBlanks.hint: Gece gec saatlere kadar calismak — yag

fillBlanks.sentence: This new AI tool is a real game ___.
We finally see ___ to eye on the budget.
Don't drop the ___ on this deadline.
A lot of hard work happens behind the ___.

multipleChoice.question: 'Get the ball rolling' ne anlama gelir?
'We're on the same page' ne demek?
Resmi bir sozlesmede hangi ifade daha uygundur?
Hangi deyim 'hemen ise koyulmak' anlamina gelir?
'Take it with a grain of salt' ne demek?
'A ballpark figure' ne tur bir sayi?
'Keep someone in the loop' ne demek?
'A piece of cake' ne anlama gelir?

matching.left: take it with a grain of salt
at the end of the day

sentenceOrder.correct: Let's get the ball rolling on the project
We need to think outside the box
Please keep me in the loop about updates
The deal fell through so we're back to square one
She hit the ground running on her first day
Can you give me a ballpark figure for the budget
At the end of the day quality matters most
We had to bite the bullet and start over
The new feature is a real game changer
I told a joke to break the ice

translation.english: Let's get the ball rolling on the new project.
acceptAlso: Let's get the ball rolling on the project

translation.turkish: Devam etmeden once hepimiz ayni fikirde olalim.
translation.english: Let's make sure we're all on the same page before we proceed.
acceptAlso: Before we proceed, let's make sure we're on the same page

translation.turkish: Kaliteden kisma — sonra bize mal olur.
translation.english: Don't cut corners on quality — it will cost us later.

translation.turkish: Beni herhangi bir degisiklik hakkinda bilgilendirmeye devam et.
translation.english: Keep me in the loop about any changes.
acceptAlso: Please keep me in the loop about any changes

translation.turkish: Proje butcesi icin yaklasik bir rakam verebilir misin?
translation.english: Can you give me a ballpark figure for the project budget?
acceptAlso: Could you give me a ballpark figure for the project budget

translation.turkish: Gozumuzu kararlamaliyiz ve yeni bir sistem satin almaliyiz.
translation.english: We need to bite the bullet and buy a new system.
acceptAlso: We have to bite the bullet and invest in a new system

translation.turkish: Gunun sonunda musteri memnuniyeti en onemli seydir.
translation.english: At the end of the day, customer satisfaction is the most important thing.
acceptAlso: At the end of the day, customer satisfaction matters most

translation.turkish: Perde arkasinda cok fazla calisma oluyor.
translation.english: A lot of work happens behind the scenes.
acceptAlso: There's a lot of work behind the scenes

translation.turkish: Bu yapay zeka araci gercek bir oyun degistirici.
translation.english: This AI tool is a real game changer.
acceptAlso: This AI tool is a game changer

translation.english: We finally see eye to eye on the budget.
acceptAlso: We finally agree on the budget
